reasoning_model_1: The model demonstrates perfect content accuracy, reciting only the target sentence '我就是觉得这个方案最好'. Regarding the core evaluation dimension 'Stress' (重音) on the word '就是' (jiùshì): The model explicitly adheres to the command. From timestamp 00:00 to 00:02, the speaker introduces a deliberate pause before '就是', significantly elongates the duration of the word, and increases its intensity compared to the surrounding text. This results in a very clear, emphatic stress that aligns perfectly with the user's requirement. The audio is fluent and free of noise.
reasoning_model_2: The model fails strictly on content accuracy. While it speaks the target sentence at the beginning, it hallucinates/adds a significant amount of unrequested text ('真的没什么好犹豫的...', etc.) continuing until 00:11, violating the script constraints. Regarding the core evaluation dimension 'Stress' on '就是': The model fails to apply the requested paralinguistic feature. In the segment 00:00-00:01, the phrase '我就是觉得' is spoken very rapidly with a flat prosody. The word '就是' receives no emphasis—no change in pitch, duration, or loudness—and sounds like a standard unstressed function word. The model completely ignores the specific instruction to stress this word.
model_comparison: Model 1 is the clear winner. It strictly follows the content constraints and successfully executes the paralinguistic instruction. The stress on '就是' in Model 1 is prominent and achieved through clear elongation and prosodic weight (00:01-00:02). In contrast, Model 2 fails on both content and feature dimensions. It hallucinates a long extension to the script and fails to place any emphasis on the target word '就是', rushing through it at the very beginning (00:00). Model 1 followed all instructions; Model 2 did not.
